# Disease resistance breeding

**Disease resistance breeding** is the process of selective breeding to produce or improve [disease resistance](/wiki/Disease_resistance "Disease resistance"). It is also used more generally for breeding for [disease tolerance](/wiki/Disease_tolerance "Disease tolerance").

Types include:

--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://image.slideserve.com/514117/breeding-for-disease-resistance-some-examples-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A presentation slide titled "Breeding for disease resistance: Some examples" outlines topics including the context, cost of disease, definition of disease resistance, measurement and genetic variability of resistance, prospects for genetic improvement, and a conclusion.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://image4.slideserve.com/9141959/plant-breeding-for-disease-resistance-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the concept of plant breeding for disease resistance, highlighting how breeding and developing resistant cultivars can reduce crop loss and dependence on fungicides and insecticides in tropical climates.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/breedingfordiseaseresistance-150121055608-conversion-gate02/95/breeding-for-disease-resistance-by-sajad-4-638.jpg?cb=1421819801
DESCRIPTION: A flowchart illustrates the steps in breeding for disease resistance, starting with the source for resistance, followed by methods of breeding, testing for resistance, and finally selecting resistant lines.

DESCRIPTION: None

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/20-140521040004-phpapp01/95/breeding-approaches-towards-disease-resistance-in-livestocks-14-638.jpg?cb=1513667202

enriched_points.expanded_content: Disease resistance breeding is the selective cultivation of plants to enhance their genetic ability to withstand diseases, reducing pathogen growth and impact.
This breeding strategy is crucial for sustainable agriculture, as it minimizes reliance on chemical pesticides and promotes healthier ecosystems while improving crop yields.
By developing disease-resistant crops, this approach directly contributes to food security, ensuring stable food supplies in the face of climate change and increasing global populations.
